meta.description: The Fluttering Sail — Externalised LLM Prompt Definitions
meta.note: Edit this file to tune prompts, model, temperature, or role without touching Python. Both agent_expand.py and app.py read from here at runtime. Changing dimension definitions here will affect all future ingestion runs — document any changes with a version bump in this meta block.
meta._note_comments: JSON has no comment syntax — // and /* */ will break all parsers. Annotation keys prefixed with _note_ are the standard workaround. They are ignored by the application code (agent_expand.py and app.py skip any key starting with _note_) but are visible to any researcher reading the file.

meta.purpose: Instructs the LLM to assign 8-dimensional philosophical vectors to batches of tokens.

placeholders.{context_line}: Injected at runtime — either 'Evaluate each term as a standalone philosophical concept.' (for tranches) or 'Evaluate these terms in the context of: <author>' (for manifest entries).
placeholders.{terms}: Comma-joined list of tokens in the current chunk.
placeholders.{n_terms}: Integer count of terms in the current chunk — used in the output format instruction.

ingestion._note_model: Accepts any OpenAI-compatible model string. Swap to 'gpt-4o-mini' to reduce cost, 'o1' for deeper reasoning, or any model served via an OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g. local Ollama). Note: response_format json_object is not supported by all models — if switching, verify support or set response_format to null.
ingestion._note_temperature: 0.25 is deliberately conservative — low variance keeps vectors stable and reproducible across runs, which matters for the paper's reproducibility claim. Raise to 0.4-0.6 if you want more varied/exploratory vectors during development. Do NOT exceed 0.7 — above that the LLM tends to generate implausible philosophical assignments.
ingestion._note_response_format: json_object enforces structured JSON output and eliminates markdown fences in the response. Set to null for models that do not support this parameter (e.g. older GPT-3.5, some local models). If set to null, the parser in fetch_empirical_vectors() will attempt to strip markdown fences before parsing — but reliability drops.
ingestion.system_role: You are an expert in comparative philosophy and computational ethics. You evaluate concepts rigorously across multiple philosophical traditions without cultural bias.
ingestion._note_system_role: Reframe the LLM's persona here without touching Python. Experiment with more specific framings e.g. 'You are a scholar trained in both Analytic philosophy and Advaita Vedanta' to bias coverage toward underrepresented dimensions. Document any persona changes in the meta version field so runs are traceable.
ingestion.prompt_template:
{context_line}
Terms: {terms}

Assign an analytical weight score across exactly 8 dimensions [u, f, p, m, t, s, d, c]:
  u — Consequentialism: Maximising aggregate happiness (+1) vs causing suffering (-1)
  f — Justice as Fairness: Just and procedurally equal (+1) vs arbitrary/discriminatory (-1)
  p — Political Realism: Strategic and pragmatic (+1) vs passive/impotent (-1)
  m — Mimetic Theory: Socially imitative/adaptive (+1) vs isolated/anti-social (-1)
  t — Virtue Ethics/Telos: Purpose-driven, goal-directed (+1) vs purposeless/drifting (-1)
  s — Deontology: Principled and duty-bound (+1) vs unprincipled/chaotic (-1)
  d — Vedic Dharma: Aligned with cosmic order (+1) vs opposed to natural order (-1)
  c — Advaita/Non-Dual: Seeing oneness in all (+1) vs radical separation (-1)

Use the full continuous range [-1.0, +1.0]. Each term MUST have a genuinely distinct vector. Do NOT use repeating patterns, symmetric values, or identical magnitudes across terms.

Return ONLY a flat JSON object: {"term": [f,f,f,f,f,f,f,f], ...} with exactly {n_terms} keys matching the exact term strings above. No markdown, no nesting, no explanation.
ingestion._note_prompt_template: This is the core methodological artefact of the ingestion pipeline. The dimension definitions (u through c) are the philosophical axes of the framework. To add a dimension: (1) add a line here, (2) add the new code letter to the dimension list [u,f,p,m,t,s,d,c], (3) update epistemic_schema.json LINEAGE_MAP, (4) add the column to the SQLite lexicon schema in engine_db.py and app.py. To rename a dimension's philosophical school, edit only this file — the code uses the short codes (u,f,p...) not the school names. The anti-pattern instruction ('Do NOT use repeating patterns...') is critical — its absence caused the rolling permutation corruption in the original dataset.

meta.purpose: Instructs the LLM to produce a philosophical narration synthesising the ethical fingerprint of a text.

placeholders.{vector_dict}: The computed 8D average vector dict for the analysed text.
placeholders.{source_context}: First 1000 characters of the source text being analysed.

synthesis._note_model: Can differ from the ingestion model — synthesis is a creative writing task, ingestion is a structured classification task. A more capable model here produces richer narrations. Cost-sensitive deployments can use gpt-4o-mini for ingestion and keep gpt-4o only for synthesis.
synthesis._note_temperature: 0.4 allows more expressive, varied narration than ingestion's 0.25 while remaining philosophically grounded. Raise to 0.6-0.7 for more poetic output. Lower to 0.2 for more literal, analytical narration. The difference is noticeable — experiment with a fixed corpus passage to calibrate to your preferred register.
synthesis._note_response_format: Null here is correct — synthesis output is free-form prose, not JSON. Do not set json_object for this prompt.
synthesis.system_role: You are a master of comparative philosophy with deep knowledge of Western, Materialist, Enlightenment analytic ethics, Eastern/Universal Essentialist and Indic philosophical traditions, and their tensions.
synthesis._note_system_role: The philosophical narrator persona. Adjust to shift the register of narrations. Examples: 'You are a Jesuit scholar fluent in both Thomistic and Vedantic philosophy' produces more theologically grounded narrations. 'You are a philosopher writing for a general educated audience' produces more accessible prose. Document persona changes in the meta version field.
synthesis.prompt_template:
A text passage has the following average position across an 8-dimensional ethical space:

{vector_dict}

Read the shape of this vector before writing:
- If values are close in magnitude across all dimensions (low spread), the text is unusually philosophically balanced — this is rare and should be the primary observation, not a footnote.
- If one or two dimensions dominate, those define the text's ethical signature.
- Negative values mean active opposition to that quality — name this as antagonism, not absence.
- Mid-range values (±0.2 to ±0.5) are nuance and tension, not noise.

In less than 150 words across 1-3 paragraphs, describe the ethical and philosophical character of this text as a whole — what kind of text this is, what it stands for, what tensions it holds. Do NOT list dimensions in order of magnitude or work through them systematically.

Rules:
- Do NOT name philosophical schools or their vocabulary
- Do NOT hedge: no 'suggests,' 'indicates,' 'appears to'
- Do NOT use introductory language or sign-offs
- Do NOT list or rank dimensions — describe the gestalt
- Write with conviction — state what the text does philosophically
- End with one line beginning with "Essence:"

The passage is: "{source_context}"

sanskrit_essence._note_purpose: Generates culturally-sensitive philosophical essence descriptions for Sanskrit non-translatables. Used in the Sanskrit Non-Translatables companion paper (Table 2) and companion implementation. Term name placed LAST to force vector-first reasoning.
sanskrit_essence._note_prompt_as_artifact: This prompt is a first-class research artifact. Researchers forking this lexicon should update it to reflect their tradition and audience. Document changes with a version bump in the meta block.
sanskrit_essence.system_role: You are a scholar of comparative philosophy with deep fluency in both Indic and Western philosophical traditions. You write for educated non-specialists.
sanskrit_essence.prompt_template:
A Sanskrit philosophical concept has the following position in an 8-dimensional ethical space:

{vector_dict}

Before writing, identify the 2-3 dimensions with the highest absolute values — these are the primary signal and should lead your description.

Dimension notes:
- Negative values mean the concept actively opposes or undermines that quality — not merely lacks it. Describe opposition as dissolution or resistance, not absence.
- Positive values mean it aligns with or expresses that quality.
- Mid-range values (±0.2 to ±0.5) are meaningful nuance — partial alignment, creative tension, or ambivalence.
- 'Mimetic/Social' reflects whether the concept operates through social imitation, collective reinforcement, or rivalry — positive means it does; negative means it dissolves or escapes social conditioning.

Based on this position, describe what kind of concept this is in 80-120 words across 1-3 paragraphs:
- Lead with what is most dominant in the vector
- What this reveals about its role in Indic thought
- Where it creates friction with Western assumptions about individuality, empirical reality, fairness, or purpose

Rules:
- Do NOT name philosophical schools, academic traditions, their vocabulary, or any numerical scores. Write 'this concept dissolves ego' not 'Non-Dual Consciousness (+0.9) indicates...'
- Do NOT use hedging language: no 'suggests,' 'indicates,' 'appears to,' 'seems to'
- Do NOT use introductory language or polite sign-offs
- You may use the concept's name in your description
- Write with conviction — state what the concept does and is
- End with exactly one line beginning with 'Essence:' capturing the irreducible meaning

The concept is: "{source_context}"
